title: Stillwater Walk
desc: The forest's morning circuit feels less like upkeep than reverence.

steps.text:
First light does not wake the Forest of Life. It finds the place already waiting. The leaves are wet but not with fresh rain. The roots are warm beneath the moss. Every branch seems paused at the exact point it most wanted to occupy, held there by whatever power keeps this forest outside ordinary time.

A quiet OverWorld warden meets you at the edge of the root court carrying shallow stone bowls in one arm and a rack of hanging water chimes in the other. "Morning circuit," she says. "The forest likes to see the light arrive properly. We help with that." Her tone makes clear that this is not superstition and not exactly maintenance either.

The circuit splits at the first arching root. One path leads through the lower court where reflection bowls must be set into their carved sockets. The other climbs toward the canopy walk where drip chimes measure which branches are calm enough for visitors.

options.label: Carry the reflection bowls through the root court
Take the water chimes up to the canopy walk

steps.text:
The root court is all curves and filtered gold, an open chamber formed by trunks too broad to feel like ordinary trees. You kneel at each carved socket, lower a bowl into place, and pour a skin of clear water until the surface settles flat. One by one the bowls catch the first sunlight and throw it upward in soft, wavering shards.

As the light spreads, nearby vines ease away from a path that had seemed closed when you arrived. Broad leaves angle themselves just enough to brighten the stone. Nothing rushes. Nothing grows. The whole court simply accepts illumination with an old, measured grace. The warden watches the reflections reach the underside of a high branch and gives a small nod. "There. Now it can see itself." 

By the time you finish the circuit, the forest feels even more impossible than before. Not louder, not grander, just more complete. The still water, the unaging leaves, the sense of a place perpetually at its chosen moment - all of it settles into you as something both peaceful and unnerving.

options.label: Stand a moment in the reflected light

steps.text:
The canopy stairs are narrow and damp, woven between roots that climb like columns through the green. Up here the air is cooler. Mist hangs in long pale ribbons and every branch seems close enough to touch, though the drop through the leaves reminds you that the forest is larger than the ground ever suggests.

You hang the water chimes one by one from hooks cut into living wood. Each chime is a tiny cup balanced over a bronze tongue. When enough dew gathers and falls, it rings. The notes do not sound random. They answer one another across the canopy walk, telling the wardens which railings are dry, which platforms are slick, and which bends of the path the forest is not in the mood to share yet.

When the full line begins to sing, the effect is delicate and enormous at the same time. Beneath you stretches a forest where nothing is born and nothing dies, and above you hangs a pattern of clear tones made by water deciding when to let go. The warden smiles faintly at the sound. "That means visitors can take the east branch today. Lucky for them."

options.label: Listen until the whole walk is singing
